hypothesis: If a CNN (e.g., EfficientNet-B0) is first pretrained on a data-rich set of additive-free NCM SEM images and then fine-tuned on a small additive-induced target set, then it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 for nine-class NCM composition/aging classification than models trained only on the target data or without pretraining, because transferable SEM morphological representations (e.g., particle cracking and surface boundaries) learned from the source domain generalize to the target domain.
